title: Parity result state + per-dimension result + finding (Phase 3a)
description: The result-state enum and the structures that carry it: per-dimension results and machine-readable findings. See SEMANTICS.md sec 6 (states) and sec 7 (dimensions). NO JSON FLOATS anywhere (canon v1 sec 6 forbids them): confidence/score fields are string-decimals.

result_state.title: result_state
result_state.description: The verbatim v1 contract enum. pass: in-scope check applicable, fact observed, no violation. fail: in-scope violation OBSERVED. unknown: in-scope and applicable, but the required fact was NOT OBSERVABLE in this E(C) (data absent) -- the anti-silent-pass guard. unsupported: the CHECKER cannot ask the question (capability gap: unmapped kind, dimension not implemented for this entity class). not_applicable: no subject exists (vacuous; labeled so it is not counted as an evaluated pass).

string_decimal.title: string_decimal
string_decimal.description: A decimal in [0,1] as a STRING (canon forbids JSON floats). Pattern allows 0, 1, and 0.xxx forms.

finding_id.description: Stable id of this finding: canon semantic-content digest over the explicit content subset {dimension, result_state, code, subject} (NOT the whole record -- message/evidence_refs/confidence/waiver_ref are excluded so a re-worded message or an added waiver does not mint a new finding identity). Named-subset preimage, like waiver_id, to avoid relying on canon sec 8 for a nested id.

code.description: A stable machine code for the finding class, e.g. missing_desired_element, structural_mismatch, prohibition_violated, prohibition_unobservable, unmapped_realized_kind, unmapped_desired_kind, ambiguous_kind_alignment, refinement_below_frontier, permitted_freedom, correspondence_ambiguous, capability_missing.

desired_logical_id.description: idmodel Tier-1 logical_id of the desired element, if any.

realized_occurrence_id.description: idmodel Tier-2 occurrence_id of the realized fact, if any.

message.description: Human-readable explanation (NOT hashed-meaning-bearing on its own; the code+subject are the load-bearing identity).

evidence_refs.description: Span-anchored / digest-anchored evidence pointers (SSRF-safe refs, not payloads).

confidence.description: Optional. STRING decimal only; never a JSON float.

waiver_ref.description: If this finding is currently suppressed by a waiver, the waiver_id. A waiver does NOT change result_state to pass; it only records suppression (SEMANTICS.md sec 8).

checks_evaluated.description: Count of in-scope checks evaluated for this dimension. A real JSON integer (canon sec 6 supports arbitrary-precision integers as JSON integers; the `$int`-tag is the test-vector encoding only, never the data model). Only FLOATS are forbidden, not integers.

finding_ids.description: finding_ids contributing to this dimension's state.

title: finding_set
description: The machine-readable finding set emitted by N(). Referenced by certificate.findings_ref.

overall_result_state.description: Aggregated per the severity rule in SEMANTICS.md sec 6.4: fail if any dimension fails; else unknown if any unknown; else unsupported if any unsupported; else pass. not_applicable is neutral (does not count).

dimension_results.description: Exactly one entry per parity_dimension (all five always present; a dimension with no subject reports not_applicable).

vacuity.description: OPTIONAL. Present (and is_vacuous=true) ONLY when the certify VACUITY TRIPWIRE fired: E(C) had NO kind-aligned realized boundary, so there was no real code to certify and overall_result_state is not_applicable across every dimension. This block is the load-bearing DISTINCTION from an ordinary single-revision not_applicable; it makes a refusal-over-noise honest rather than a fake-green. Absent on every non-vacuous finding_set (so existing artifacts are byte-identical). NOT part of the finding_set_id preimage (which digests {dimension_results, findings} only); pinned instead by the certificate digest.

reason.description: Machine code for why the certify was vacuous, e.g. no_kind_aligned_realized_boundary.

realized_boundary_count.description: Total E(C) boundary count (may be >0 even when vacuous — e.g. config-file noise boundaries).

kind_aligned_realized_count.description: Count of E(C) boundaries that map to a desired kind via kind-alignment; 0 is exactly the vacuity condition.
